operation: First, a new 2D sketch object is initialized. A circle feature with a radius of 3 units is added to the sketch. Next, a location transformation is defined with a position vector of (10, 10, 0), corresponding to a 10 unit offset along the X-axis, 10 unit offset along the Y-axis, and 0 offset along the Z-axis relative to the default origin. Finally, the created circle sketch is translated to the defined new location to produce the final positioned 2D sketch geometry.
geometry: This is a flat planar 2D circular shape lying entirely on the XY coordinate plane. Its center is offset from the global origin, positioned at positive X and positive Y coordinates relative to the origin, with no thickness or depth, forming a simple closed curved 2D profile.
function: This is a pre-positioned 2D circular base sketch designed to be used as a foundational profile for subsequent 3D modeling operations such as extrusion, cutting, or as an alignment reference marker in larger CAD designs or assemblies.
